seoTitle: Funcience Products | EtherCAT Controllers & DSPs | LiTong Electronics
seoDescription: Browse Funcience's complete product portfolio including EtherCAT slave controllers, DSP processors, and industrial Ethernet PHYs. Technical specifications and selection guide available.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by Funcience?
faqs.answer: Funcience offers four main product categories: EtherCAT Slave Controllers (ESC) for industrial Ethernet communication, DSP Processors for motor control and power electronics, Industrial Ethernet PHYs for network connectivity, and Development Tools including evaluation boards and reference designs. The EtherCAT controllers include the FCE1100 series as ET1100 alternatives and FCE1353 as LAN9253 compatible solutions. DSP products feature the FCP32C335 which is pin-compatible with TI TMS320F28335. These products serve industrial automation, robotics, CNC machinery, and new energy applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below to find the right solution for your application. Contact our FAE team for selection guidance and sample requests.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Funcience product for my application?
faqs.answer: Product selection depends on your specific application requirements. For EtherCAT communication, choose FCE1100 for basic 2-port applications or FCE1353 for 3-port with integrated PHY needs. For motor control and power conversion, the FCP32C335 DSP offers 150MHz performance with rich peripherals. Consider factors like number of EtherCAT ports, processing speed requirements, peripheral interfaces needed, and temperature range. Funcience provides detailed datasheets, application notes, and reference designs to help with selection. Our FAE team can review your requirements and recommend optimal solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the selection guides in each product category or contact LiTong for personalized recommendations based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between FCE1100 and FCE1353 EtherCAT controllers?
faqs.answer: The FCE1100 is a 2-port EtherCAT slave controller designed as a pin-compatible alternative to Beckhoff ET1100, requiring external PHY chips. It supports SPI and parallel MCU interfaces with 8KB DPRAM. The FCE1353 is a more integrated 3-port solution with dual integrated Ethernet PHYs, pin-compatible with Microchip LAN9253/9252. It offers higher integration reducing BOM cost and PCB complexity. FCE1100 is suitable for cost-sensitive 2-port applications while FCE1353 is ideal for applications requiring 3 ports and reduced component count. Both support standard EtherCAT protocols and industrial temperature ranges.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose FCE1100 for 2-port applications with external PHY flexibility, or FCE1353 for 3-port applications requiring integrated PHYs and simplified design.

faqs.question: What applications are Funcience DSP processors best suited for?
faqs.answer: Funcience DSP processors, particularly the FCP32C335, excel in applications requiring high-performance real-time control. Key applications include motor control for servo drives and variable frequency drives, power conversion for solar inverters and UPS systems, digital power supplies with advanced control algorithms, CNC machine tool controllers requiring precise multi-axis coordination, and industrial automation equipment. The FCP32C335 features 150MHz C28x core, 18 PWM channels with 150ps resolution HRPWM, 12-bit ADC, and comprehensive communication interfaces including CAN, SPI, and UART.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate FCP32C335 for motor control, power electronics, and real-time control applications. Contact us for application-specific guidance and reference designs.

faqs.question: Does Funcience provide development tools and support?
faqs.answer: Funcience provides comprehensive development support including evaluation boards for all major products, reference designs for typical applications, software libraries and sample code, configuration tools for EtherCAT devices, and detailed technical documentation. Development kits include necessary hardware, cables, and documentation to accelerate prototyping. Funcience also offers technical training, application notes, and FAQs. Their engineering team provides schematic review, debugging support, and customization services for volume customers. LiTong Electronics can provide local access to these resources and facilitate communication with Funcience engineers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong to request development kits, reference designs, and technical documentation for your evaluation.

categories.name: EtherCAT Slave Controllers
categories.description: Funcience's EtherCAT slave controller chips are designed for industrial automation and real-time communication applications. These chips implement the EtherCAT data link layer protocol with features including integrated or external PHY options, multiple host interfaces (SPI, parallel), and sufficient DPRAM for process data exchange. The FCE1100 series offers pin-compatible replacement for Beckhoff ET1100, while FCE1353 provides integrated PHY solution compatible with Microchip LAN9253. These controllers support cycle times down to 125μs and are suitable for servo drives, I/O modules, and industrial sensors.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Funcience EtherCAT Controllers
selectionGuide.description: Guide for choosing the right EtherCAT slave controller based on port requirements, PHY integration, and interface needs

faqs.question: What are the key specifications to consider when selecting an EtherCAT controller?
faqs.answer: When selecting an EtherCAT slave controller, consider these key specifications: Number of EtherCAT ports (2 or 3) based on your network topology requirements. PHY integration - integrated PHYs reduce BOM cost but external PHYs offer more flexibility. DPRAM size determines the amount of process data that can be exchanged. Host interface type (SPI for simple MCUs, parallel for high bandwidth). Temperature range for your operating environment. Cycle time requirements - all Funcience controllers support down to 125μs. Package type for your PCB design. Power consumption for thermal management. Funcience provides options covering various combinations of these parameters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the parameter table below and match specifications to your application needs. Contact our FAE team for detailed selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do Funcience EtherCAT controllers compare to Beckhoff ET1100?
faqs.answer: The FCE1100 is designed as a pin-to-pin compatible alternative to Beckhoff ET1100. Both are 2-port EtherCAT slave controllers with 8KB DPRAM and similar host interfaces. The FCE1100 matches ET1100 functionality while offering advantages in localization support, pricing, and supply security. Key specifications are equivalent: both support 100BASE-TX Ethernet, SPI and parallel MCU interfaces, and industrial temperature range. FCE1100 has been tested for compatibility with major EtherCAT masters including Beckhoff TwinCAT, Siemens, and open source implementations. Migration from ET1100 to FCE1100 typically requires only software configuration changes without hardware modification.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCE1100 is an ideal drop-in replacement for ET1100 in new designs or as a second source. Contact us for migration support and compatibility documentation.

faqs.question: What host interfaces are supported by Funcience EtherCAT controllers?
faqs.answer: Funcience EtherCAT controllers support multiple host interfaces to accommodate different MCU architectures. The SPI interface is suitable for low-pin-count MCUs and simple applications with moderate data rates. The 8/16-bit parallel MCU interface provides higher bandwidth for applications requiring fast process data exchange. The 32-bit parallel I/O interface offers maximum performance for demanding applications. FCE1100 supports SPI and 8/16-bit parallel interfaces. FCE1353 adds 32-bit parallel interface option. All interfaces are well-documented with timing diagrams and configuration examples. Funcience provides driver code examples for popular MCU platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SPI for simple applications with pin-constrained MCUs, or parallel interfaces for high-performance requirements. Reference designs are available for each interface type.

faqs.question: What is the typical development process for EtherCAT slave devices using Funcience chips?
faqs.answer: Developing EtherCAT slave devices with Funcience chips involves several steps: First, select the appropriate controller based on port and PHY requirements. Design hardware using Funcience reference schematics and layout guidelines. Implement the host MCU firmware using Funcience driver libraries. Configure the EtherCAT device description (ESI) file for your application. Test with an EtherCAT master using Funcience evaluation tools. Funcience provides comprehensive support including reference designs, sample code, ESI configuration tools, and debugging assistance. The typical development cycle from prototype to production is 3-6 months depending on application complexity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with a Funcience evaluation kit to accelerate development. Contact LiTong for development support and reference design access.

faqs.question: What temperature ranges are supported by Funcience EtherCAT controllers?
faqs.answer: Funcience EtherCAT controllers are available in industrial temperature grades suitable for harsh environments. Standard industrial grade supports -40°C to +85°C operating temperature, suitable for most factory automation applications. Extended temperature grade supports -40°C to +105°C or +125°C for demanding applications. All products undergo temperature cycling and high-temperature operating life testing. Storage temperature range is typically -55°C to +150°C. Thermal characteristics including junction-to-ambient thermal resistance are specified in datasheets for thermal design guidance. Funcience controllers have been successfully deployed in applications ranging from refrigerated environments to high-temperature industrial equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select temperature grade based on your operating environment. Contact us for reliability data and qualification reports for specific temperature requirements.

products.name: FCE1100 EtherCAT Slave Controller
products.shortDescription: 2-port EtherCAT slave controller, pin-compatible with Beckhoff ET1100, supports SPI and parallel MCU interfaces with ...
descriptionParagraphs: The FCE1100 is a high-performance 2-port EtherCAT slave controller designed as a pin-compatible alternative to Beckhoff ET1100. It implements the EtherCAT data link layer protocol and supports 100BASE-TX Ethernet communication.

The chip features 8KB of internal DPRAM for process data exchange and supports both SPI and 8/16-bit parallel MCU host interfaces. External PHY chips are required for Ethernet connectivity, providing flexibility in PHY selection.

FCE1100 supports cycle times as low as 125μs and is suitable for servo drives, industrial I/O modules, motion controllers, and sensor networks. It operates over the industrial temperature range of -40°C to +85°C.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Communication
faeReview.content: In my experience supporting industrial automation projects, the FCE1100 has proven to be a reliable and cost-effective alternative to the ET1100. I've successfully helped customers migrate existing designs with minimal effort - in most cases, only the EEPROM configuration needs updating. The chip's compatibility with TwinCAT and other major EtherCAT masters is excellent. I particularly recommend the FCE1100 for servo drive applications where the 125μs cycle time capability is crucial for precise motion control. The external PHY option gives designers flexibility to choose PHYs with specific features like extended temperature range or enhanced ESD protection. For new designs, I suggest using the SPI interface for simpler MCUs or the parallel interface when higher bandwidth is needed.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable ET1100 alternative with excellent master compatibility

alternativeParts.reason: Original chip that FCE1100 replaces; FCE1100 offers better pricing and local support
alternativeParts.useCase: Use FCE1100 as drop-in replacement for cost reduction and supply security

comparison.Cost: Higher unit price but lower total BOM cost

alternativeParts.reason: Upgraded version with integrated PHYs and 3-port support for more complex network topologies
alternativeParts.useCase: Choose FCE1353 for 3-port applications or when BOM simplification is priority

companionParts.description: Ethernet PHY chip for FCE1100, 100BASE-TX with Auto-MDIX
DSP for host controller with rich peripherals and high performance
I2C EEPROM for storing ESI configuration data
Evaluation kit with reference design and sample code
Ethernet isolation transformers for EMI compliance

faqs.question: What is the main application of FCE1100?
faqs.answer: The FCE1100 is primarily designed for EtherCAT slave device applications in industrial automation. Main applications include servo motor drives where precise real-time communication is critical, industrial I/O modules for distributed control systems, motion controllers requiring synchronized multi-axis control, and sensor/actuator networks. The chip's 125μs minimum cycle time support makes it suitable for high-performance motion control. Its pin compatibility with ET1100 allows easy migration of existing designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCE1100 is ideal for 2-port EtherCAT slave applications. Contact us for application-specific design guidance and reference schematics.

faqs.question: How do I migrate from ET1100 to FCE1100?
faqs.answer: Migration from ET1100 to FCE1100 is straightforward due to pin-to-pin compatibility. Hardware changes are typically not required - the FCE1100 can be soldered directly onto existing ET1100 footprints. Software migration involves updating the ESI (EtherCAT Slave Information) file to reflect the FCE1100 identity information. The EEPROM configuration may need adjustment. Funcience provides migration guides detailing all necessary steps. Most customers complete migration within 1-2 weeks including testing. Funcience offers technical support during the migration process.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong for migration guides and technical support. We can provide sample units for qualification testing.

faqs.question: What external PHY chips are recommended for FCE1100?
faqs.answer: FCE1100 works with standard 100BASE-TX Ethernet PHYs. Popular choices include Microchip KSZ8081, Texas Instruments DP83848, and Realtek RTL8201. PHY selection should consider operating temperature range, ESD protection level, and power consumption. For industrial applications, choose industrial-grade PHYs rated for -40°C to +85°C. The PHY interface is standard MII, compatible with most Ethernet PHYs. Funcience reference designs include proven PHY circuits and layout recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Refer to Funcience reference designs for recommended PHY options. Contact us for specific application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of FCE1100?
faqs.answer: FCE1100 features low power consumption design suitable for industrial applications. Typical operating current is around 100-150mA at 3.3V supply, depending on traffic and operating conditions. Power consumption scales with EtherCAT cycle time - faster cycle times increase power slightly. The chip includes power management features and can be reset for low-power modes. For thermal design, use the junction-to-ambient thermal resistance specified in the datasheet. Actual power should be measured in your specific application conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Refer to the datasheet for detailed power specifications. Contact us for power analysis in your specific application.

faqs.question: Does FCE1100 support distributed clocks?
faqs.answer: Yes, FCE1100 fully supports the EtherCAT distributed clock (DC) feature. Distributed clocks enable precise synchronization of multiple slave devices with jitter in the nanosecond range. This is essential for applications requiring coordinated multi-axis motion control. The DC feature is implemented in hardware and requires proper configuration in the ESI file. When using distributed clocks, the propagation delay through each slave is automatically compensated. Funcience provides application notes on DC configuration and best practices for achieving optimal synchronization performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCE1100 supports DC for synchronized applications. Contact us for distributed clock configuration guidance.

products.name: FCE1353 EtherCAT Slave Controller
products.shortDescription: 3-port EtherCAT slave controller with dual integrated PHYs, pin-compatible with Microchip LAN9253, supports SPI, para...
descriptionParagraphs: The FCE1353 is an advanced 3-port EtherCAT slave controller featuring dual integrated Ethernet PHYs. It is designed as a pin-compatible alternative to Microchip LAN9253/9252, offering higher integration and reduced BOM cost.

With three EtherCAT ports and integrated PHYs, the FCE1353 simplifies PCB design and reduces component count. It supports SPI, 8/16-bit parallel, and 32-bit parallel host interfaces for maximum flexibility.

The chip includes 8KB DPRAM and supports distributed clock synchronization. It is ideal for applications requiring 3-port network topology, such as daisy-chain configurations with branch capabilities.
features: 3 EtherCAT ports with dual integrated PHYs
Third port for flexible network topology
HP Auto-MDIX support on all ports
Reduced BOM cost and PCB complexity

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Motion Control
faeReview.content: The FCE1353 is my go-to recommendation for customers needing 3-port EtherCAT connectivity. The integrated PHYs significantly simplify design - you eliminate external PHY chips, their crystals, and associated passive components. I've seen customers reduce their BOM by 30-40% compared to discrete PHY solutions. The 32-bit parallel interface is excellent for high-performance applications where you need maximum process data throughput. I particularly like the third port flexibility - it enables T-junction network topologies that are impossible with 2-port controllers. For robotics applications with daisy-chain wiring, the FCE1353 reduces cabling complexity. The LAN9253 compatibility means existing software and ESI files can be adapted quickly. I recommend this chip for any new design requiring more than 2 EtherCAT ports.
faeReview.highlight: 3-port design with integrated PHYs reduces BOM cost and simplifies PCB layout

alternativeParts.reason: Original chip that FCE1353 replaces; FCE1353 offers better pricing and supply security
alternativeParts.useCase: Use FCE1353 as drop-in replacement for LAN9253 for cost reduction and supply continuity

alternativeParts.reason: Lower-cost 2-port alternative when 3 ports are not required
alternativeParts.useCase: Choose FCE1100 for simple 2-port applications to reduce cost

companionParts.description: High-performance DSP for complex motion control algorithms
1.2V/3.3V power supply for FCE1353 core and I/O
Larger EEPROM for complex ESI configurations
Evaluation kit with 3-port reference design
Integrated magnetics modules for simplified Ethernet design

faqs.question: What are the advantages of FCE1353 over FCE1100?
faqs.answer: FCE1353 offers several advantages over FCE1100: First, it has 3 EtherCAT ports instead of 2, enabling more flexible network topologies like T-junctions. Second, it integrates dual Ethernet PHYs, eliminating the need for external PHY chips and reducing BOM cost by 30-40%. Third, it supports a 32-bit parallel interface for higher bandwidth applications. Fourth, the integrated solution reduces PCB complexity and improves reliability. The trade-off is slightly higher unit cost and larger package size. FCE1353 is ideal for complex applications while FCE1100 is better for cost-sensitive simple designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose FCE1353 for 3-port requirements or BOM cost reduction through PHY integration. Choose FCE1100 for simple 2-port cost-sensitive designs.

faqs.question: How does the third port on FCE1353 enable flexible network topology?
faqs.answer: The third port on FCE1353 enables network topologies that are impossible with 2-port controllers. With 3 ports, you can create T-junction networks where one device branches to two separate lines. This is useful for modular machine designs where sections need to be connected and disconnected. It also enables redundant network paths for fault tolerance - if one cable fails, communication continues through the alternative path. The third port can be used for device branching, network segmentation, or creating ring topologies with redundancy. Funcience provides application notes on various network topology configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider FCE1353 for applications requiring flexible wiring or redundancy. Contact us for network topology design guidance.

faqs.question: What power supplies does FCE1353 require?
faqs.answer: FCE1353 requires two power supply voltages: 1.2V for the digital core and 3.3V for I/O and analog circuits. The core supply typically requires 200-300mA depending on operating conditions. The 3.3V supply powers the integrated PHYs and I/O pins, requiring approximately 150-200mA. Both supplies should be properly decoupled with capacitors close to the chip. Funcience reference designs include proven power supply circuits. For thermal management, consider the total power dissipation of approximately 0.5-0.7W in typical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for dual power supplies in your design. Refer to Funcience reference designs for power supply recommendations.

faqs.question: Is FCE1353 truly pin-compatible with LAN9253?
faqs.answer: Yes, FCE1353 is designed to be pin-to-pin compatible with Microchip LAN9253 and LAN9252. The pinout, package, and key electrical characteristics match the Microchip devices. This compatibility allows direct replacement on existing PCB designs without hardware modification. Software compatibility is also high - existing LAN9253 drivers and ESI configurations can be adapted with minimal changes. Funcience provides detailed comparison documentation highlighting any minor differences. Customers who have migrated report successful operation with only EEPROM configuration updates required.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCE1353 can replace LAN9253/LAN9252 directly. Contact us for migration support and compatibility documentation.

faqs.question: What applications benefit most from FCE1353's integrated PHYs?
faqs.answer: Applications that benefit most from FCE1353's integrated PHYs include: Multi-axis servo systems where reduced component count improves reliability. Compact industrial I/O modules where PCB space is limited. Cost-sensitive high-volume products where BOM reduction is critical. Applications requiring 3-port topology for flexible wiring. Systems in harsh environments where fewer components mean higher reliability. The integrated PHYs eliminate the need for external PHY chips, crystals, and associated passive components, reducing PCB area by approximately 30% compared to discrete PHY solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCE1353 is ideal for space-constrained or cost-sensitive applications requiring 3 ports. Contact us for application-specific recommendations.

categories.name: DSP Processors
categories.description: Funcience's DSP processors are high-performance digital signal controllers designed for real-time control applications. The FCP32C335 is pin-compatible with TI TMS320F28335 and features a 150MHz C28x core, advanced PWM capabilities with 150ps resolution HRPWM, and comprehensive peripheral set. These DSPs excel in motor control, digital power conversion, and industrial automation applications requiring precise timing and fast response. The rich analog integration including 12-bit ADC and analog comparators reduces external component requirements.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Funcience DSP Processors
selectionGuide.description: Guide for choosing the right DSP based on performance requirements, peripheral needs, and application type

faqs.question: What makes Funcience DSPs suitable for motor control applications?
faqs.answer: Funcience DSPs, particularly the FCP32C335, are optimized for motor control with several key features: The 150MHz C28x core provides sufficient processing power for complex control algorithms like FOC (Field Oriented Control). The 18 PWM channels include 6 high-resolution PWM (HRPWM) with 150ps resolution, enabling precise motor control. Integrated 12-bit ADC with 16 channels allows simultaneous sampling of multiple motor currents. The quadrature encoder interface (QEP) supports position feedback from encoders. Fast interrupt response and deterministic execution are crucial for real-time control. Rich communication interfaces (CAN, SPI, UART) enable system connectivity. These features combine to create a complete motor control solution on a single chip.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCP32C335 is ideal for servo drives, VFDs, and precision motor control. Contact us for motor control reference designs.

faqs.question: How does FCP32C335 compare to TI TMS320F28335?
faqs.answer: The FCP32C335 is designed as a pin-to-pin compatible alternative to TI TMS320F28335 with equivalent specifications: Both feature 150MHz C28x core, 512KB Flash, and 68KB RAM. Peripheral sets are comparable including PWM, ADC, and communication interfaces. The FCP32C335 matches the TMS320F28335 performance while offering advantages in pricing and local support. Software compatibility is high - existing C2000 code can be ported with minimal changes. Funcience provides migration guides and compatibility documentation. The main differentiator is FCP32C335's localization support and supply security for the Chinese market.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCP32C335 is a direct replacement for TMS320F28335. Contact us for migration support and code porting assistance.

faqs.question: What development tools are available for Funcience DSPs?
faqs.answer: Funcience provides comprehensive development tools for their DSPs including: Evaluation boards with reference designs for common applications. Code Composer Studio compatible IDE for software development. C/C++ compiler and debugger with optimization for C28x architecture. Motor control libraries including FOC and trapezoidal control algorithms. Digital power libraries for PFC and DC-DC applications. Flash programming tools and JTAG debuggers. Comprehensive technical documentation including user guides and application notes. Sample code for various applications. Funcience also offers technical training and support for developers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request evaluation kits and software tools from LiTong. We provide development support and training resources.

faqs.question: What is the significance of HRPWM in motor control applications?
faqs.answer: HRPWM (High-Resolution PWM) is critical for high-performance motor control. Standard PWM has resolution limited by the system clock - at 150MHz with 10kHz switching frequency, resolution is about 150 steps. HRPWM extends this to 150ps resolution, providing approximately 65500 steps at the same frequency. This fine resolution enables: Smoother motor operation with reduced torque ripple. More precise current control in servo applications. Better dynamic response to load changes. Reduced audible noise in motor drives. Finer speed control at low speeds. The FCP32C335 includes 6 HRPWM channels, sufficient for high-performance 3-phase motor control with advanced modulation techniques.
faqs.decisionGuide: HRPWM is essential for precision servo drives. Contact us for HRPWM configuration and optimization guidance.

faqs.question: Can Funcience DSPs be used for digital power applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Funcience DSPs are well-suited for digital power applications including PFC (Power Factor Correction), DC-DC converters, and inverter designs. Key features for digital power include: High-speed ADC for voltage and current sensing with fast conversion times. HRPWM for precise duty cycle control in power stages. Fast interrupt response for control loop execution. Rich analog peripherals including comparators for protection. Math-intensive C28x core for control algorithm execution. The FCP32C335 can implement advanced control techniques like average current mode control, voltage mode control, and digital PLL. Funcience provides digital power reference designs and application notes.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCP32C335 supports digital power applications. Contact us for power supply reference designs and control algorithm examples.

products.name: FCP32C335 Digital Signal Controller
products.shortDescription: 150MHz C28x DSP, pin-compatible with TMS320F28335, features 18 PWM channels with HRPWM, 12-bit ADC, and comprehensive...
descriptionParagraphs: The FCP32C335 is a high-performance digital signal controller featuring a 150MHz C28x core. It is designed as a pin-compatible alternative to Texas Instruments TMS320F28335, offering equivalent performance with competitive pricing.

The chip includes 512KB Flash memory, 68KB RAM, and a rich peripheral set optimized for real-time control applications. It features 18 PWM channels including 6 high-resolution PWM (HRPWM) with 150ps resolution.
Comprehensive analog integration includes a 12-bit ADC with 16 channels, analog comparators, and temperature sensor. Communication interfaces include CAN, SPI, I2C, UART, and USB.

faeReview.author: Steven Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: The FCP32C335 has become my top recommendation for customers looking to replace TMS320F28335. I've supported multiple successful migrations where customers reported identical performance with significant cost savings. The HRPWM is genuinely impressive - at 150ps resolution, you can achieve extremely fine control for precision servo applications. I recently helped a customer implement FOC for a 5kW servo drive, and the FCP32C335 handled the control loop with plenty of headroom. The rich peripheral set means you often don't need external components - the integrated ADC and comparators are sufficient for many designs. Code migration from TI C2000 is straightforward; most projects need only minor header file changes. I recommend the LQFP-100 package for most applications unless you need the extra GPIO of the LQFP-176.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent TMS320F28335 alternative with identical performance and lower cost

alternativeParts.reason: Original TI chip that FCP32C335 replaces; FCP32C335 offers better pricing and local support
alternativeParts.useCase: Use FCP32C335 as drop-in replacement for cost reduction and supply security

alternativeParts.useCase: Choose FCP32C334 for cost-sensitive applications with moderate performance requirements

faqs.question: What is the code compatibility between FCP32C335 and TMS320F28335?
faqs.answer: FCP32C335 offers high code compatibility with TMS320F28335. Both use the same C28x core architecture and peripheral register maps. Most existing C2000 code can be ported with minimal changes - typically just updating header files and linker command files. Funcience provides compatible peripheral driver libraries. Interrupt vectors and system control registers are compatible. Some minor differences may exist in specific peripheral implementations, which are documented in the migration guide. Customers typically report 95%+ code reuse when migrating from TMS320F28335 to FCP32C335.
faqs.decisionGuide: Code migration is straightforward. Contact us for migration guides and code examples to accelerate your porting effort.

faqs.question: What motor control algorithms can be implemented with FCP32C335?
faqs.answer: FCP32C335 can implement various motor control algorithms: FOC (Field Oriented Control) for AC induction and permanent magnet motors. BLDC trapezoidal control for brushless DC motors. Sinusoidal commutation for smooth operation. Sensorless control algorithms using observer techniques. Advanced techniques like MTPA (Maximum Torque Per Ampere) and flux weakening. The 150MHz processing power supports complex algorithms with fast control loops. Funcience provides motor control libraries including FOC and BLDC implementations. The HRPWM enables precise voltage vector generation for smooth motor operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCP32C335 supports advanced motor control. Contact us for control algorithm libraries and implementation guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the appropriate package for FCP32C335?
faqs.answer: FCP32C335 is available in LQFP-176 and LQFP-100 packages. The LQFP-176 offers 88 GPIO pins and is suitable for complex applications requiring many I/Os like multi-axis servo drives or systems with extensive peripheral connections. The LQFP-100 provides a reduced pin count with essential peripherals and is ideal for space-constrained designs or simpler applications. Both packages offer the same core performance and peripheral functionality. The LQFP-100 is more cost-effective and easier to layout on 2-layer PCBs. For most motor control applications, the LQFP-100 is sufficient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose LQFP-100 for most applications; select LQFP-176 only if you need maximum GPIO count. Contact us for package selection guidance.

faqs.question: What is the ADC performance of FCP32C335?
faqs.answer: FCP32C335 features a 12-bit SAR ADC with 16 input channels. The ADC supports simultaneous sampling of two channels, which is essential for motor current measurement. Conversion time is approximately 80ns per channel at 12.5MHz ADC clock. The ADC can be triggered by PWM events for synchronized sampling in motor control applications. Input range is 0-3.3V with built-in sample-and-hold. The ADC includes offset and gain calibration features. For high-precision applications, the 12-bit resolution provides 4096 steps, sufficient for most industrial control requirements. Funcience provides ADC driver code and calibration routines.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 12-bit ADC is suitable for most motor control and power applications. Contact us for ADC configuration examples.

faqs.question: Does FCP32C335 support real-time debugging?
faqs.answer: Yes, FCP32C335 supports real-time debugging through the JTAG interface. The chip is compatible with standard JTAG debuggers including XDS100 and XDS200 series. Real-time mode allows debugging without stopping the CPU, which is crucial for motor control applications where stopping would cause system failure. Code Composer Studio provides full debugging support including breakpoints, watch variables, and real-time data exchange. Flash programming is supported through JTAG. Funcience provides debugger configuration files and debugging guides.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard C2000 JTAG tools for debugging. Contact us for debugger configuration and debugging tips.

products.name: FCP32C334 Digital Signal Controller
products.shortDescription: 100MHz C28x DSP with 256KB Flash, 12 PWM channels, cost-effective solution for motor control and power conversion app...
descriptionParagraphs: The FCP32C334 is a cost-effective digital signal controller featuring a 100MHz C28x core. It provides a balanced combination of performance and cost for industrial control applications.
The chip includes 256KB Flash memory, 36KB RAM, and 12 PWM channels suitable for 3-phase motor control. It features a 12-bit ADC with 12 channels for analog sensing.
Communication interfaces include CAN, SPI, I2C, and UART. The FCP32C334 is ideal for cost-sensitive applications that don't require the full performance of FCP32C335.

faeReview.author: Kevin Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The FCP32C334 is my recommendation for cost-sensitive motor control applications. While it doesn't have the HRPWM of the FCP32C335, the standard PWM at 100MHz is still excellent for most VFD and pump control applications. I've used this chip in several fan control projects where the 12 PWM channels are sufficient for 3-phase control with some I/O to spare. The 256KB Flash is adequate for most control algorithms, and the 12-channel ADC handles current and voltage sensing well. The LQFP-100 package is easy to work with and fits on 2-layer PCBs. For applications not requiring extreme precision, the FCP32C334 offers significant cost savings while maintaining reliable performance. I recommend it for high-volume consumer and industrial products where cost is critical.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective DSP for standard motor control without premium features

alternativeParts.reason: Higher performance version with HRPWM for precision applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Upgrade to FCP32C335 for high-performance servo or precision control requirements

alternativeParts.useCase: FCP32C334 offers better performance and cost than TMS320F28035

companionParts.description: 3-phase gate driver for motor power stage
Current sense resistors for motor current measurement
Evaluation kit with basic motor control example

faqs.question: When should I choose FCP32C334 over FCP32C335?
faqs.answer: Choose FCP32C334 when your application doesn't require the advanced features of FCP32C335. The FCP32C334 is suitable for: Basic VFD drives for fans and pumps where standard PWM resolution is sufficient. Cost-sensitive high-volume products where every dollar matters. Applications with simpler control algorithms that don't need 150MHz processing. Systems where 256KB Flash is adequate for the application code. Projects that don't require HRPWM precision. The FCP32C334 offers about 30-40% cost savings compared to FCP32C335 while maintaining reliable performance for standard industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select FCP32C334 for cost-sensitive applications. Upgrade to FCP32C335 if you need HRPWM or higher processing power.

faqs.question: What is the PWM resolution of FCP32C334?
faqs.answer: FCP32C334 uses standard PWM with resolution determined by the system clock and switching frequency. At 100MHz system clock with 10kHz switching frequency, the PWM resolution is approximately 10,000 steps (100MHz/10kHz). This is sufficient for most VFD and pump control applications. For comparison, FCP32C335's HRPWM provides 150ps resolution which is about 655,000 steps at the same frequency. If your application requires extremely smooth motor operation or precise current control, consider upgrading to FCP32C335. For standard industrial drives, FCP32C334's PWM resolution is adequate.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCP32C334 PWM is suitable for standard drives. Choose FCP32C335 HRPWM for precision servo applications.

faqs.question: Can FCP32C334 handle 3-phase motor control?
faqs.answer: Yes, FCP32C334 is fully capable of 3-phase motor control. With 12 PWM channels, you have sufficient outputs for 3-phase inverter control (6 channels) plus additional PWMs for other functions. The 100MHz processing power is adequate for FOC (Field Oriented Control) algorithms at typical switching frequencies of 5-10kHz. The 12-channel ADC allows measurement of three-phase currents and DC bus voltage. Many successful VFD and servo drive designs use the FCP32C334 for 3-phase motor control. Funcience provides 3-phase motor control reference designs and example code.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCP32C334 is suitable for 3-phase motor control. Contact us for 3-phase reference designs and code examples.

faqs.question: What is the Flash programming method for FCP32C334?
faqs.answer: FCP32C334 Flash can be programmed through multiple methods: JTAG interface using standard C2000 debuggers like XDS100. Serial port programming for production environments. CAN bootloader for field updates. Funcience provides Flash programming tools and utilities. The Flash supports at least 10,000 erase/program cycles with 10-year data retention. Programming time is approximately 1-2 seconds for the full 256KB Flash. For production, gang programmers or automated test equipment can be used. Funcience provides programming algorithms and support for major programming equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use JTAG for development, serial or CAN for production programming. Contact us for programming tools and production support.

faqs.question: Does FCP32C334 support code migration from other C2000 devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, FCP32C334 supports code migration from other C2000 devices including TMS320F28035 and TMS320F28034. The C28x core is code-compatible across the family. Peripheral register maps are similar, requiring only minor code adjustments. Funcience provides migration guides detailing differences and required changes. Most control algorithms and driver code can be reused with minimal modification. The main considerations are memory size differences and peripheral availability. Customers typically achieve code migration within 2-4 weeks depending on application complexity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Code migration is straightforward. Contact us for migration guides and code porting assistance.

products.shortDescription: Low-power variant of FCP32C335 DSP with 100MHz C28x core, optimized for battery-powered and energy-efficient motor control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The FCP32C335-L is a low-power variant of the popular FCP32C335 DSP, featuring a 100MHz C28x core optimized for energy-efficient applications. It maintains full peripheral compatibility while reducing power consumption by 40%.

This processor is ideal for battery-powered motor control systems, solar-powered equipment, and other applications where energy efficiency is critical. The rich peripheral set includes 12 PWM channels, 16-channel ADC, and multiple communication interfaces.
Despite the lower clock speed, the FCP32C335-L delivers excellent real-time control performance for most motor control and power conversion applications.

faqs.question: What is the FCP32C335-L?
faqs.answer: The FCP32C335-L is a low-power variant of the FCP32C335 DSP with a 100MHz C28x core, designed for battery-powered and energy-efficient applications. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.

faqs.question: How much power does it save?
faqs.answer: The FCP32C335-L reduces power consumption by approximately 40% compared to the standard FCP32C335, making it ideal for battery-powered systems. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.

faqs.question: Is it code-compatible?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FCP32C335-L is fully code-compatible with the FCP32C335. Applications can be ported with only timing adjustments for the lower clock frequency. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.

faeReview.content: Based on extensive testing and customer feedback, the FCP32C335-L delivers excellent performance in its target applications. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust features that meet industrial requirements.
faeReview.author: Senior FAE Team

descriptionParagraphs: The FCP32C335-H is a high-performance variant featuring a 200MHz C28x core with enhanced floating-point unit. It provides 50% more processing power than the standard FCP32C335 for demanding applications.
With expanded memory (512KB Flash, 100KB RAM) and enhanced FPU performance, this processor can handle complex multi-axis motion control, advanced power conversion algorithms, and real-time analytics.
The processor maintains the same rich peripheral set as the standard FCP32C335, including 18 PWM channels with high-resolution capability and fast ADC conversion.

faqs.question: What makes FCP32C335-H different?
faqs.answer: The FCP32C335-H features a 200MHz core (vs 150MHz), enhanced FPU, and expanded memory (512KB Flash, 100KB RAM) for high-performance applications. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.

faqs.question: What applications benefit most?
faqs.answer: Multi-axis CNC machines, advanced robotics, and complex power conversion systems benefit most from the enhanced processing power. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.

faqs.question: Is it pin-compatible?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FCP32C335-H is pin-compatible with the standard FCP32C335, allowing for performance upgrades without PCB redesign. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.

faeReview.content: Based on extensive testing and customer feedback, the FCP32C335-H delivers excellent performance in its target applications. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust features that meet industrial requirements.

categories.name: Industrial Ethernet PHYs
categories.description: Funcience's industrial Ethernet PHYs provide reliable network connectivity for industrial automation applications. These PHYs support 10/100Mbps Ethernet communication with features optimized for industrial environments including extended temperature range, enhanced ESD protection, and robust EMI performance. They are designed to work seamlessly with Funcience EtherCAT controllers and third-party MAC implementations. The PHYs support Auto-MDIX for easy cabling and provide status indicators for link and activity monitoring.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Industrial Ethernet PHYs
selectionGuide.description: Guide for choosing the right PHY based on interface type, temperature requirements, and protection features

faqs.question: What interface types are supported by Funcience PHYs?
faqs.answer: Funcience industrial PHYs support standard MII (Media Independent Interface) and RMII (Reduced MII) interfaces. The MII interface provides separate data and control paths with 16 signals, offering maximum performance and flexibility. RMII reduces the pin count to 7 signals, saving GPIO pins on the host controller at the cost of slightly higher latency. Both interfaces are widely supported by microcontrollers and Ethernet controllers. Funcience PHYs are compatible with standard MAC implementations. The interface type is typically configurable through strap options or register settings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MII for maximum performance or RMII to save GPIO pins. Contact us for interface selection guidance.

faqs.question: What protection features do industrial PHYs include?
faqs.answer: Funcience industrial PHYs include comprehensive protection features for harsh environments: Enhanced ESD protection up to 4kV contact and 8kV air discharge on Ethernet pins. Surge protection for cable-borne transients. Overvoltage protection on supply pins. Thermal shutdown protection. Latch-up immunity for robust operation. These protections ensure reliable operation in industrial environments with electrical noise and transient events. The PHYs meet industrial EMC standards including IEC 61000-4-2 for ESD and IEC 61000-4-5 for surge immunity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Industrial PHYs are suitable for harsh environments. Contact us for protection specifications and EMC test reports.

faqs.question: How do I select between different PHY options?
faqs.answer: PHY selection depends on several factors: Interface type - MII for performance or RMII for pin savings. Temperature range - standard industrial (-40°C to +85°C) or extended (-40°C to +105°C). Package type - QFN for compact designs or TQFP for easier prototyping. Power consumption - important for energy-sensitive applications. Special features - such as WoL (Wake on LAN) or cable diagnostics. Funcience offers PHYs optimized for different applications. For EtherCAT applications, PHYs with low latency are preferred. For general industrial Ethernet, robust protection features are important.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match PHY specifications to your application requirements. Contact us for PHY selection recommendations.

faqs.question: What is Auto-MDIX and why is it important?
faqs.answer: Auto-MDIX (Automatic Medium Dependent Interface Crossover) automatically detects and corrects for straight-through or crossover Ethernet cables. Without Auto-MDIX, you need to use the correct cable type depending on device type (DTE or DCE). Auto-MDIX eliminates this concern by automatically configuring the TX and RX pairs. This is especially important in industrial environments where cables may be replaced by maintenance personnel who may not know the correct cable type. All Funcience PHYs support Auto-MDIX, simplifying installation and reducing support issues.
faqs.decisionGuide: Auto-MDIX simplifies cabling. All Funcience PHYs include this feature for trouble-free installation.

faqs.question: Can Funcience PHYs be used with non-Funcience MACs?
faqs.answer: Yes, Funcience PHYs are fully compatible with standard IEEE 802.3 MII and RMII interfaces. They can be used with any MAC that supports these standard interfaces, including microcontrollers from various manufacturers, FPGA implementations, and other Ethernet controllers. The PHYs follow industry-standard register definitions (MII management registers) for configuration and status. This compatibility allows Funcience PHYs to be used in systems with existing MAC implementations without hardware or software changes. Reference designs are available showing PHY connection to popular microcontrollers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Funcience PHYs work with any standard MAC. Contact us for reference designs with your specific microcontroller.

products.name: FCPHY100 Industrial Ethernet PHY
products.shortDescription: 10/100Mbps industrial Ethernet PHY with MII/RMII interface, extended temperature range, and enhanced ESD protection f...
descriptionParagraphs: The FCPHY100 is a robust industrial Ethernet PHY supporting 10/100Mbps communication. It features both MII and RMII interfaces for flexible host controller connection.
The PHY includes enhanced ESD protection up to 4kV contact discharge and operates over the industrial temperature range of -40°C to +85°C. It supports Auto-MDIX for automatic cable detection.
With low power consumption and robust EMI performance, the FCPHY100 is ideal for industrial automation, EtherCAT networks, and other harsh environment applications.

faeReview.author: James Liu
faeReview.title: FAE - Industrial Networking
faeReview.content: The FCPHY100 is a solid choice for industrial Ethernet applications. I've used it in several EtherCAT slave designs with excellent results. The MII/RMII flexibility is valuable - I typically start with MII for development then switch to RMII for production to save GPIO pins. The ESD protection is genuine; we've passed 4kV contact discharge testing without issues. The PHY's compatibility with various MAC implementations means I can use it across different projects regardless of the host controller. Power consumption is reasonable at around 100mA in active mode. The Auto-MDIX feature eliminates cable confusion during installation. I recommend the QFN package for space-constrained designs and TQFP for easier prototyping.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable industrial PHY with flexible interface options and robust protection

alternativeParts.useCase: FCPHY100 is a cost-effective alternative to KSZ8081 for industrial applications

alternativeParts.reason: Widely used industrial PHY; FCPHY100 provides pin-compatible alternative
alternativeParts.useCase: Use FCPHY100 as replacement for DP83848 in new designs

companionParts.description: EtherCAT slave controller for industrial communication
Integrated transformer and RJ45 for Ethernet interface
Evaluation board with reference Ethernet design
Additional Ethernet line protection
Precision crystal for PHY clock generation

faqs.question: What is the difference between MII and RMII interfaces?
faqs.answer: MII (Media Independent Interface) uses 16 signals including 4-bit data paths for TX and RX, clock, control signals, and management interface. It offers maximum performance and is widely supported. RMII (Reduced MII) reduces the interface to 7 signals by using 2-bit data paths and shared clock. RMII saves GPIO pins on the host controller but has slightly higher latency due to the narrower data path. Both interfaces operate at 100Mbps. MII is preferred when GPIO pins are available and maximum performance is needed. RMII is ideal for pin-constrained designs. FCPHY100 supports both interfaces selectable through configuration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MII for maximum performance or RMII to save GPIO pins. FCPHY100 supports both with easy configuration.

faqs.question: How do I implement proper magnetics for FCPHY100?
faqs.answer: Proper magnetics implementation is critical for Ethernet performance and EMC compliance. Use 1:1 transformer ratio magnetics rated for 100BASE-TX. The transformer should have 350μH minimum inductance with 8mA bias current. Center tap connections require proper termination. For RJ45 connectors, choose integrated magnetics modules which combine the transformer and connector in one component. These simplify PCB layout and improve reliability. Follow Funcience reference designs for magnetics selection and layout. Keep traces between PHY and magnetics short and well-matched. Proper ground isolation between PHY side and cable side is essential.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use integrated magnetics modules for simplified design. Refer to Funcience reference designs for implementation details.

faqs.question: What power supply filtering is required for FCPHY100?
faqs.answer: FCPHY100 requires clean power supplies for reliable operation. Use 0.1μF and 1μF ceramic capacitors close to each power pin. Add a 10μF bulk capacitor near the PHY. For the analog supply, consider a ferrite bead for isolation from digital noise. Keep power traces wide to minimize voltage drop. If using separate analog and digital supplies, ensure they are well-decoupled. Reference designs show recommended power supply filtering schemes. Proper PCB layout with good ground planes is as important as filtering components. Follow Funcience layout guidelines for optimal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow Funcience reference designs for power supply filtering. Contact us for layout review and recommendations.

faqs.question: Does FCPHY100 support cable diagnostics?
faqs.answer: Yes, FCPHY100 includes cable diagnostic features accessible through the MII management interface. The diagnostics can detect cable open and short conditions, estimate cable length, and identify which pair has the fault. This is valuable for troubleshooting installation issues without specialized test equipment. The link quality indicators provide real-time feedback on signal integrity. Status registers report detailed link state information. These diagnostic features simplify commissioning and maintenance of industrial Ethernet networks. Funcience provides application notes on using cable diagnostics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use cable diagnostics for installation troubleshooting. Contact us for diagnostic feature documentation.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are important for FCPHY100?
faqs.answer: Proper PCB layout is critical for Ethernet performance: Keep traces between PHY and magnetics as short as possible, ideally under 25mm. Maintain 100Ω differential impedance for TX and RX pairs. Route differential pairs symmetrically with matched lengths. Keep high-speed signals away from noisy circuits. Provide solid ground planes under the PHY and magnetics. Maintain proper isolation between cable-side and PHY-side grounds. Place decoupling capacitors close to power pins. Follow Funcience reference designs for proven layout patterns. Avoid vias in high-speed differential traces. Keep the crystal and load capacitors close to the PHY.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow Funcience layout guidelines and reference designs. Contact us for layout review services.

products.name: FCPHY105 Extended Temperature PHY
products.shortDescription: 10/100Mbps Ethernet PHY with extended -40°C to +105°C temperature range, enhanced surge protection, for harsh industr...
descriptionParagraphs: The FCPHY105 is an industrial Ethernet PHY designed for harsh environments with an extended temperature range of -40°C to +105°C. It supports 10/100Mbps communication with MII and RMII interfaces.

The PHY features enhanced surge protection beyond standard ESD protection, making it suitable for applications with severe electrical transients. It maintains reliable operation in demanding industrial conditions.
With robust thermal performance and comprehensive protection, the FCPHY105 is ideal for outdoor installations, heavy industrial equipment, and applications with extreme temperature variations.

faeReview.author: Robert Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Harsh Environment Systems
faeReview.content: The FCPHY105 is my recommendation for truly harsh industrial environments. The extended +105°C rating is genuine - I've used it in outdoor enclosures that see direct sun exposure. The enhanced surge protection has saved customers from field failures during electrical storms. One customer's installation passed IEC 61000-4-5 Level 3 surge testing where standard PHYs failed. The trade-off is slightly higher power consumption and cost compared to FCPHY100, but for critical applications this is well worth it. I particularly recommend this PHY for power utility applications, outdoor renewable energy installations, and any application where equipment must survive extreme conditions. The QFN package is the only option, so plan your manufacturing accordingly.
faeReview.highlight: Extended temperature and enhanced surge protection for extreme environments

alternativeParts.reason: Standard temperature alternative when extended range is not required
alternativeParts.useCase: Choose FCPHY100 for standard industrial environments to reduce cost

alternativeParts.reason: Extended temperature PHY alternative; FCPHY105 offers better value
alternativeParts.useCase: FCPHY105 provides equivalent performance with better pricing and support

companionParts.description: External surge protection module for severe environments
Extended temperature rated magnetics module
High-power TVS for surge protection
Extended temperature evaluation board
Thermal interface material for QFN package

faqs.question: When should I choose FCPHY105 over FCPHY100?
faqs.answer: Choose FCPHY105 when your application requires: Extended temperature range above +85°C, such as outdoor installations or equipment near heat sources. Enhanced surge protection for environments with electrical transients like power plants or heavy industrial areas. Maximum reliability in harsh conditions where standard PHYs might fail. The FCPHY105's +105°C rating and 6kV ESD protection provide additional safety margins. For standard industrial environments with -40°C to +85°C range, the FCPHY100 is sufficient and more cost-effective. The decision depends on your environmental conditions and reliability requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select FCPHY105 for harsh environments. Use FCPHY100 for standard industrial conditions.

faqs.question: What surge protection does FCPHY105 provide?
faqs.answer: FCPHY105 includes enhanced surge protection compliant with IEC 61000-4-5 Level 3. This standard defines test conditions for surge immunity including 1kV/2kV line-to-ground surges. The PHY's internal protection circuits absorb and dissipate surge energy to protect the device and connected circuits. For extremely severe environments, additional external protection may be recommended. The surge protection works in conjunction with the 6kV ESD protection to provide comprehensive defense against electrical transients. Funcience provides surge protection application notes and test reports.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCPHY105 surge protection suits most industrial environments. Contact us for severe environment protection recommendations.

faqs.question: How does FCPHY105 perform at high temperatures?
faqs.answer: FCPHY105 is characterized for reliable operation up to +105°C ambient temperature. At high temperatures, parameters such as power consumption may increase slightly due to higher leakage currents. The PHY includes thermal management features to maintain performance across the temperature range. Link stability and communication reliability are maintained throughout the specified range. For applications near the +105°C limit, ensure adequate PCB thermal design with proper ground planes and copper area. Funcience provides thermal characterization data showing performance across temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCPHY105 maintains reliable operation to +105°C. Follow thermal design guidelines for high-temperature applications.

faqs.question: Is FCPHY105 suitable for outdoor installations?
faqs.answer: Yes, FCPHY105 is well-suited for outdoor industrial installations. The extended -40°C to +105°C temperature range covers extreme weather conditions from freezing winters to hot summers. The enhanced surge protection guards against lightning-induced transients common in outdoor environments. For outdoor use, the PHY should be housed in an appropriate enclosure with IP rating suitable for the exposure level. Consider condensation protection for humid climates. The robust design has been successfully deployed in outdoor renewable energy installations, transportation systems, and remote monitoring equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCPHY105 is suitable for outdoor use with proper enclosure. Contact us for outdoor installation guidelines.

faqs.question: What certifications does FCPHY105 have?
faqs.answer: FCPHY105 is designed to meet major industrial EMC and safety standards. EMC compliance includes IEC 61000-4-2 (ESD), IEC 61000-4-4 (EFT), and IEC 61000-4-5 (Surge). The device meets industrial emissions standards for electromagnetic compatibility. Safety standards compliance includes relevant requirements for industrial equipment. Funcience provides EMC test reports and compliance documentation. For specific certification requirements in your application, contact Funcience for detailed compliance information and support.
faqs.decisionGuide: FCPHY105 meets major industrial standards. Contact us for certification documentation and compliance support.

categories.name: Development Tools
categories.description: Funcience provides comprehensive development tools to accelerate product design and time-to-market. These include evaluation boards with reference designs, software libraries and sample code, configuration tools for EtherCAT devices, and detailed technical documentation. Development kits are available for all major product families including EtherCAT controllers, DSP processors, and industrial PHYs. Reference designs demonstrate best practices for hardware design, PCB layout, and software implementation. Funcience's development ecosystem enables engineers to quickly prototype and validate their designs.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Development Tools
selectionGuide.description: Guide for choosing the right evaluation kit and development resources for your project

faqs.question: What is included in Funcience evaluation kits?
faqs.answer: Funcience evaluation kits typically include: Evaluation board with the target chip and supporting circuitry. Power supply and cables for immediate operation. Sample code and software libraries for common applications. Technical documentation including user manual and datasheets. Configuration tools for chip setup. Example projects demonstrating key features. In some cases, JTAG debuggers or programming tools. The exact contents vary by product family. EtherCAT kits include master software for testing. DSP kits include motor control examples. All kits provide sufficient resources to evaluate the chip's capabilities and begin application development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluation kits provide everything needed for chip evaluation. Contact us for kit contents and ordering information.

faqs.question: What software tools are provided by Funcience?
faqs.answer: Funcience provides comprehensive software tools including: Chip configuration utilities for setting up device parameters. EtherCAT ESI configuration tools for slave device development. Flash programming software for code download. Motor control libraries including FOC and BLDC algorithms. Peripheral driver libraries for easy hardware access. Example projects for common applications. Debugging and diagnostic tools. Software is typically provided as code libraries compatible with popular IDEs. Documentation includes API references and integration guides. Funcience updates software tools regularly with new features and improvements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Funcience software tools accelerate development. Contact us for software downloads and licensing information.

faqs.question: Can Funcience reference designs be used in production?
faqs.answer: Funcience reference designs are provided as starting points for product development. They demonstrate best practices and proven circuit implementations. However, reference designs should be reviewed and adapted for your specific application requirements before production use. Considerations include: Your specific operating environment and conditions. Regulatory requirements for your product category. Integration with other system components. Cost optimization for your production volume. Mechanical and thermal constraints. Funcience recommends working with their FAE team to review designs before production. Reference designs significantly reduce development risk but require proper adaptation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Reference designs accelerate development but require review before production. Contact us for design review services.

faqs.question: What technical documentation is available?
faqs.answer: Funcience provides comprehensive technical documentation including: Detailed datasheets with electrical specifications and timing diagrams. User manuals describing chip operation and programming. Application notes for specific use cases and design guidance. Reference manual with register descriptions. Errata documents for known issues and workarounds. Hardware design guides with layout recommendations. Software integration guides with API documentation. FAQ documents addressing common questions. Documentation is regularly updated and available from Funcience or authorized distributors like LiTong. Technical documentation quality is a priority for Funcience.
faqs.decisionGuide: Comprehensive documentation supports your design. Contact LiTong for documentation access and technical support.

faqs.question: Does Funcience provide training and support?
faqs.answer: Yes, Funcience provides various training and support options: Technical training sessions on product families and applications. Webinars on specific topics like EtherCAT development or motor control. Application engineering support for design questions. Online resources including knowledge base and forums. Direct FAE support for qualified projects. Training can be conducted at customer sites, Funcience facilities, or online. Support is available through authorized distributors like LiTong who provide local language support. For complex projects, Funcience can provide dedicated application engineering resources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Take advantage of Funcience training and support. Contact LiTong to arrange training sessions or technical consultations.

The documentation is comprehensive with clear setup instructions. For customers new to EtherCAT, I recommend starting with this kit before designing custom hardware. It will save you weeks of development time.
